characteristics.label: Necessary
characteristics.description: The requirement defines a capability, characteristic, or constraint that is genuinely needed; removing it would leave a deficiency.
characteristics.reason: Necessity is a stakeholder-value judgement. Whether a requirement is truly needed cannot be decided by a local deterministic heuristic without domain knowledge; source traceability (a structural proxy already enforced elsewhere) shows a requirement HAS an origin, not that it is NEEDED. Declaring this covered would simulate coverage.

characteristics.label: Appropriate
characteristics.description: The requirement is stated at the right level of abstraction: it says what is needed, free of premature implementation or design detail.
characteristics.reason: Implementation-leak detection (flagging technology, storage, or UI-mechanic terms inside a requirement statement) is feasible with a deterministic term heuristic but is not yet implemented. It is an honest gap, not out of scope.

characteristics.label: Unambiguous
characteristics.description: The requirement has only one possible interpretation; it avoids vague, subjective, or unanchored wording.

characteristics.label: Complete
characteristics.description: The requirement fully states the needed capability with no missing information the reader must supply.
characteristics.reason: Semantic completeness of a requirement against real-world need requires domain knowledge no local deterministic heuristic has. Structural markers ([PENDING INPUT], scaffolding score) flag KNOWN gaps but cannot decide that an otherwise-populated statement is complete. Declaring this covered would simulate coverage (adversarial seed doc 39 §4.6).

characteristics.label: Singular
characteristics.description: The requirement states a single capability; it does not combine multiple requirements into one statement.

characteristics.label: Feasible
characteristics.description: The requirement can be realized within known constraints (technical, cost, schedule, regulatory) with acceptable risk.
characteristics.reason: Feasibility needs technical, cost, and schedule judgement about a specific solution context. No local deterministic heuristic can decide it; declaring it covered would simulate coverage.

characteristics.label: Verifiable
characteristics.description: The requirement is stated so that its satisfaction can be checked by inspection, analysis, demonstration, or test, with a measurable or observable acceptance criterion.

characteristics.label: Correct
characteristics.description: The requirement accurately represents the stakeholder need it is meant to capture.
characteristics.reason: Correctness is agreement with the real stakeholder need and requires stakeholder validation, not a local heuristic over the text. Declaring it covered would simulate coverage.

characteristics.label: Conforming
characteristics.description: The requirement follows an approved template, pattern, or standard for its statement (e.g. an EARS pattern).
